
Created outline

Searched web for "Mureka AI music API documentation platform.mureka.ai developer REST API"

Created outline

Searched web for "site:mureka.ai API instrumental generation endpoint bearer token example"

Yes, that's the one! Mureka (platform.mureka.ai) — not Mubert. Good catch. They have:

Live public API with Bearer token auth
Instrumental generation (no vocals) — perfect for campaign background music
Models: Mureka O1 (latest, highest quality) and V7.5
Same async poll pattern as HeyGen/RunwayML (submit → poll → download)
This is a direct drop-in for Suno. Want me to:

Replace 

suno-generator.ts
 with a real mureka-generator.ts wired to platform.mureka.ai
Update SUNO_CONFIG → MUREKA_CONFIG in 

media-pipeline-config.ts
Add 'mureka' to GeneratorServiceEnum in schema
Update the test page card and audio test route to use it
Add MUREKA_API_KEY to the env check
You'll need an API key from platform.mureka.ai — do you have one already, or do you want me to wire it up first and you grab the key after?

I got the key, but I did not add a payment card! This thing is expensive!!


Searched web for "Mureka AI music API pricing credits free tier cost per generation platform.mureka.ai"

Yeah — the official platform.mureka.ai API starts at $1,000/month for B2B. That's enterprise pricing, not for us.

Here's the real picture for this use case:

Option	Cost	Status
Mureka official API	$1,000/mo minimum	❌ Way too expensive
Mureka consumer ($10/mo)	~$0.025/track	✅ Cheap — but no API access, just web UI
Mubert API	~$0.10–$0.30/track, pay-as-you-go	✅ Real API, perfect for ambient loops
Replicate (MusicGen)	~$0.01/track (Meta's open model)	✅ Cheapest, you already have Replicate-style billing
My actual recommendation: Mubert (api.mubert.com). It's purpose-built for exactly what you need — royalty-free ambient instrumental loops for landing pages. Pay-as-you-go, clean REST API, no $1K minimums.

That said — this is a low-priority feature. Theme music is nice-to-have: the campaign works 100% without it. The test card already returns a 501 Not Implemented gracefully.

My suggestion: Leave it as-is for now and come back when you're ready to wire up Mubert. The generator slot is clean and waiting — just a key swap in the config.

Want to move on to something more impactful, or shall I implement Mubert now?

here is Mubert.com!
{"method":"","error":{"code":102,"desc":"Wrong request"},"api_ver":"api_1.2.0"}
